title: 10 best herbal teas for nerve pain

remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian tea's sedative properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing inflammation and numbness associated with nerve pain.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian tea's muscle relaxant properties can help to relieve spasms and cramps caused by nerve damage or compression, providing relief from shooting pains.
Analgesic properties: Valerian tea contains analgesic compounds that can help to reduce the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from chronic nerve pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, teas helps with nerve pain because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system. The root of the valerian plant contains compounds that have a sedative effect, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate nerve pain. Valerian teas also contain antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation, a common cause of nerve pain. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, valerian teas can provide relief from nerve pain and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, has been shown to inhibit the activity of GABA receptors, which can help regulate nerve pain by reducing excitability in nerve cells.
Isovaleryl valerianate: Isovaleryl valerianate, a sesquiterpene ester, has been found to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce nerve pain and inflammation.
Valerenol: Valerenol, a sesquiterpene alcohol, has been shown to have anxiolytic and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate nerve pain and anxiety associated with it.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound responsible for the herb's sedative and analgesic properties.
Stems: The stems are often used in combination with the roots, as they contain similar compounds that contribute to the tea's therapeutic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature and the roots are fully developed.
Clean the harvested valeriana officinalis roots thoroughly with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned valeriana officinalis roots in a warm dry place away from direct sunlight for several weeks.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder to desired consistency.
Steep 1 teaspoon of ground valeriana officinalis in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to make a soothing tea.

remedy_properties: Passionflower flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to reduce anxiety, stress, and inflammation, which can contribute to nerve pain relief by calming the nervous system.
Tetrahydroalkaloids (THAs): THAs in Maypop tea have been found to interact with GABA receptors, leading to a sedative effect that can help calm irritated nerves and reduce nerve pain.
Anthocyanins: The antioxidant properties of anthocyanins may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with nerve damage, which can contribute to chronic nerve pain relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, teas helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids and alkaloids that interact with the nervous system. The tea's active compounds, such as passiflorine and flavonoids, have been shown to possess anxiolytic and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with nerve damage. Additionally, the tea's sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension, further contributing to its pain-relieving properties.
remedy_constituents: Isopassiflorin: A flavonoid glycoside that helps with nerve pain by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can lead to a decrease in neuronal excitability and pain transmission.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with nerve damage.
Passifloric acid: A flavonoid acid with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its pain-relieving effects by reducing inflammation and modulating pain signaling pathways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata are commonly used to make teas for nerve pain due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic effects.
Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata are used in teas for nerve pain due to their ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety through their sedative properties.
Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata are used to make teas for nerve pain due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves and store in airtight container.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a teapot and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried flowers in the boiled water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh filter and discard solids.
Drink the tea immediately and repeat as needed up to three times daily.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve nerve pain by reducing inflammation and numbing the affected area.
Adhyperforin: Similar to hyperforin, adhyperforin also exhibits analgesic properties, which may help alleviate nerve pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain.
Hypericin: This pigment has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect nerves from damage and reduce oxidative stress that can contribute to nerve pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, teas helps with nerve pain because it contains hyperforin and hypericin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in damaged nerve tissue, alleviating pain and discomfort. Additionally, the tea's bioflavonoids and phenolic acids may also contribute to its analgesic effects, making it a popular natural remedy for nerve pain and other conditions.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones: These constituents, particularly hypericin and pseudohypericin, have potent anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system.

Phenolic acids: Flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and myricetin, in Hypericum perforatum exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help mitigate nerve damage, reduce pain, and promote healing in nerve tissue.

Flavonoids: Hyperforin, a flavonoid in Hypericum perforatum, has shown significant analgesic and anti-inflammatory activity, which helps relieve nerve pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body and reducing inflammation in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate nerve pain.
Flowers: They contain hypericin and hyperforin, bioactive compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects on nerve pain.
Roots: They are rich in alkaloids, including hypericin and pseudohypericin, which have been traditionally used to treat nerve pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers for a standard batch.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Steep 2-3 teaspoons of the powder in a teapot with 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup for 2 minutes.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before consumption.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, which may contribute to nerve pain relief.

Neuroprotective compounds: The tea contains neuroprotective compounds like flavonoids and terpenes that may help protect and preserve nerves from damage, promoting healthy nerve function and potentially alleviating nerve pain.

Vasodilation: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may increase blood flow to the affected areas, which could help reduce numbness, tingling, and pain associated with nerve damage or compression.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with nerve pain because it improves blood flow and oxygenation to the brain and nerves. The tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in damaged nerve tissues. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids and terpenoids have a neuroprotective effect, which helps to shield nerve cells from damage and promote their growth. This ultimately leads to reduced nerve pain and improved overall nerve function.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in nerve cells, alleviating pain associated with conditions like neuropathy.
Terpenoids: Specifically, ginkgolides and bilobalides, help protect nerve cells from damage, improve blood flow, and reduce inflammation, which in turn reduces nerve pain.
Bilobalide: A terpenoid compound found in Ginkgo biloba, bilobalide has been shown to have neuroprotective effects and reduce excitotoxicity in nerve cells, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba for teas due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to their pain-relieving properties.
Seeds: Seeds are another widely used part of Ginkgo biloba, particularly for their potential in reducing inflammation and nerve pain.
Roots: Roots of Ginkgo biloba are also used in teas, as they contain compounds that may help alleviate nerve pain and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in early autumn when leaves are fully formed.
Clean and dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a warm place for 2-3 days.
Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the dried leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes or until desired strength is achieved.
Strain the tea and serve immediately to release the herbal benefits of ginkgo biloba.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
Corticosteroid-like compounds: Turmeric tea contains corticosteroid-like compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nerve pain and swelling.
Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in turmeric tea, such as sesquiterpenes and terpenoids, have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve nerve pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, teas helps with nerve pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric tea has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to nerve pain. Additionally, curcumin has been found to have neuroprotective effects, promoting the health and function of nerve cells. This makes turmeric tea a potential natural remedy for alleviating nerve pain and promoting overall nerve health. Regular consumption may provide relief from chronic nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin is a phenolic compound that helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC is a phenolic compound that helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the activity of pain-sensing receptors, thereby reducing the transmission of pain signals to the brain.

Cucurbitacin E: Cucurbitacin E is a triterpenoid compound that helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are commonly used to make teas for nerve pain because they contain high concentrations of curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are also used to make teas for nerve pain due to their rich content of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate nerve discomfort and inflammation.

Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa can be used to make teas for nerve pain as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to soothe nerve pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 1-2 teaspoons of dried curcuma longa root in cold water for 30 seconds to remove impurities.
Boil 1 liter of water in a kettle or on the stovetop for 5-7 minutes to reach optimal temperature.
Steep the curcuma longa root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the root fragments and sediment.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before serving to enhance its flavor and aroma properties.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties in the herbal blend could help reduce nerve inflammation, which is often a contributing factor to nerve pain. This property may help alleviate symptoms such as numbness, tingling, or burning sensations associated with nerve pain.

Antioxidants and free radical scavengers: Antioxidants present in the tea could neutralize free radicals that can damage nerves and contribute to chronic pain conditions like neuropathy. By reducing oxidative stress, these antioxidants might help alleviate nerve pain symptoms.

Calcium channel blockers: Certain herbs in the blend, such as passionflower or lemon balm, have calcium channel-blocking properties that may help reduce the transmission of painful signals along nerve fibers. This property could potentially provide relief from nerve pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, teas helps with nerve pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The capsaicin in Capsicum annuum interacts with the body's pain receptors, blocking the production of a chemical called substance P. This chemical is responsible for sending pain signals to the brain. By inhibiting its production, Capsicum annuum teas can effectively reduce nerve pain and inflammation, providing relief for individuals suffering from conditions like neuropathy, shingles, and multiple sclerosis. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to traditional pain management treatments.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps with nerve pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing pain perception.

Capsaicinoids (including dihydrocapsaicin and nordihydrocapsaicin): Capsaicinoids help with nerve pain by inhibiting the activity of certain ion channels, such as TRPV1, which are involved in pain transmission, and also by releasing endorphins, which are natural painkillers.

Flavonoids (including kaempferol and quercetin): Flavonoids help with nerve pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are factors that contribute to the development and maintenance of nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for nerve pain due to their high content of capsaicin, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: Used to make teas for nerve pain due to their high content of capsaicin, which can help reduce pain and inflammation.
Seeds: Used to make teas for nerve pain due to their high content of capsaicin, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and flowers when they are at their peak of potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at a low temperature of 30-40 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Crush 2-3 grams of dried capsicum annuum leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powdered plant material in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the pulp.

remedy_properties: Apigenin: A flavonoid compound found in chamomile tea that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce nerve pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
Apiole: A sesquiterpene lactone in chamomile tea that has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm and relax nerves, reducing anxiety and pain associated with nerve damage.
Alpha-hydroxy acids (AHAs): Natural humectants present in chamomile tea that help to soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by nerve pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with nerve pain because it contains apigenin and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms that can contribute to nerve pain. The soothing and calming effects of chamomile also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can exacerbate nerve pain. By addressing the underlying causes of nerve pain, chamomile teas can provide relief and comfort, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with nerve damage and discomfort.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to soothe and calm nerve pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
α-Bisabolol oxide B: This sesquiterpene has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain perception.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make teas for nerve pain due to their high content of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas for nerve pain due to their soothing and calming effects, which can help alleviate nerve discomfort and promote relaxation.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make teas for nerve pain due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in a clean container for tea preparation.
Measure 1 teaspoon of the dried flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove the solids.
Serve the tea warm or at room temperature after it has cooled for a few minutes.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol, a primary constituent of peppermint oil, helps to numb the skin and relieve itching, burning, and tingling sensations associated with nerve pain, providing temporary relief.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint tea, such as menthone and limonene, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling around damaged nerves, alleviating pressure on the nervous system.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol found in peppermint tea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the nervous tissue from oxidative damage and reduce pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with nerve pain because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's high concentration of menthol and menthone helps to block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from nerve pain. Additionally, peppermint's ability to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms can help to alleviate tension that may be contributing to nerve pain. By promoting relaxation and reducing pain, peppermint tea can provide a natural and effective solution for managing nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with nerve damage.
L-menthone: A monoterpene present in peppermint, it has a mild analgesic effect and can help reduce pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties that may help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and promoting the regeneration of nerve cells.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are the most used part for teas due to their high concentration of essential oils, which are responsible for the herb's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Mentha x piperita stems are used for teas as they contain a higher concentration of menthone, a compound that helps alleviate nerve pain and inflammation.
Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are also commonly used for teas due to their high menthol content, which provides a cooling sensation and helps numb nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and 1 tablespoon of dried flowers for a standard serving size.
Steep the fresh leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their oils and flavor.
Combine the steeped leaves with the dried flowers and let it infuse for an additional 2 to 3 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids and separate the liquid.
Serve the tea hot or chilled depending on your preference and enjoy the refreshing herbal flavor.

remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, in Kansas Coneflower tea have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, alleviating nerve pain.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, like beta-amyrin and oleanolic acid, in Kansas Coneflower tea have analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, which can directly target and alleviate nerve pain by reducing transmission of pain signals to the brain.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, in Kansas Coneflower tea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect against damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, ultimately contributing to nerve pain relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with nerve pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea contains compounds like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and echinacoside, which work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the nervous system. By modulating the body's natural response to pain, Echinacea angustifolia tea may provide relief for individuals suffering from conditions such as neuropathy, shingles, or fibromyalgia, promoting a sense of calm and comfort. Regular consumption may also enhance nerve function and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, particularly echinacoside, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
Alkylamides: These bioactive compounds have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate nerve pain and discomfort.

Polysaccharides: The polysaccharide components of Echinacea angustifolia, particularly beta-glucans, have immunomodulatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain by modulating the immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the most commonly used part, as they contain high concentrations of compounds like alkylamides and polyacetylenes, which are believed to help alleviate nerve pain.
Barks: The barks are used due to their potential analgesic properties, which may help soothe and reduce nerve pain.
Leaves: Leaves are used in some teas because they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain, although they are less potent than roots.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30-60 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and 10-20 grams of dried flowers.
Crush the dried roots into smaller pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Combine the crushed roots and flowers in a heat-resistant container with 1 liter of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to create a potent herbal infusion.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Brahmi tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and terpenes, which reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with nerve damage or compression.

Antioxidant activity: The high antioxidant content in Brahmi tea helps protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to nerve pain and dysfunction.

Neuroprotective compounds: Brahmi tea contains neuroprotective compounds like acetylcholine, which helps regulate neurotransmitter function, modulate pain perception, and promote nerve health, thereby reducing symptoms of nerve pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri, also known as brahmi, teas helps with nerve pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and inflammation in the nervous system. The plant contains compounds that promote the growth of new neurons and improve communication between nerve cells. This leads to improved nerve function and reduced pain perception. Additionally, Bacopa monnieri has antioxidant properties that protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, further contributing to its pain-relieving effects and promoting overall nerve health.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: These triterpenoid saponins help with nerve pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, thereby alleviating pain and promoting nerve regeneration.

Bacopaside II: This bacoside is known for its neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.

Monnierin: This alkaloid has been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nerve pain by blocking the production of pain mediators and reducing inflammation in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bioactive compounds, including bacosides, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Barks: They contain alkaloids and saponins that have been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for nerve pain relief.
Roots: They are a primary source of bacosides, which have been shown to have neuroprotective and anti-anxiety effects, helping to alleviate nerve pain and other symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers and store them in a cool dry place.
Measure 1 teaspoon of the herb for every 8 ounces of water to achieve the desired potency.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a saucepan over low to medium heat for about 5 minutes.
Steep the herb in the heated water for 5-10 minutes to allow for maximum extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then let it cool before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Malkangani teas helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique compound called alkannin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. This compound works by reducing swelling in the affected area and blocking the production of pain signals to the brain, thereby providing effective relief from chronic nerve pain. Additionally, Malkangani tea's antioxidant properties help protect nerves from damage caused by oxidative stress, further enhancing its pain-relieving benefits.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: Malkangani tea helps to reduce nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds and releasing natural analgesics, thereby reducing swelling and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Malkangani tea help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to nerve cells, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy nerve function.

Neuroprotective properties: The bioactive compounds in Malkangani tea have been shown to stimulate the production of neurotransmitters that help regulate pain perception, while also protecting against neurodegenerative diseases such as neuropathy.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Bark: The bark contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried celastrus paniculatus powder into a small teapot or cup.
Boil 250ml of filtered water in a kettle and pour it over the powder.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the herbs to infuse.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately, enjoying its calming effects on your nerve endings.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony teas helps with nerve pain because of its unique combination of botanical compounds. The tea's main active ingredient, paeoniflorin, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, which are known to exacerbate nerve damage and inflammation. Additionally, the tea's flavonoids have been found to possess antioxidant properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy nerve function. This natural synergy may help to alleviate symptoms of nerve pain, providing relief for individuals suffering from conditions such as sciatica, shingles, and diabetic neuropathy.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Peony tea helps to relieve muscle spasms and cramps caused by nerve pain, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peony tea reduce swelling and irritation in the affected areas, which can help alleviate nerve pain caused by inflammation.
Sedative property: Peony tea's sedative effects help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may exacerbate nerve pain, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Peony plant are rich in alkaloids and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and numbness associated with nerve pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the Chinese Peony contain a compound called cinnamic acid, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried paeonia lactiflora root extract in doses of 2-4 grams per serving.
Measure one teaspoon of the root extract and combine it with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any sediment or particles.
Drink the tea immediately, or store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Galangal teas helps with nerve pain because its active compound, gingerols, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and irritation in the nervous system. Additionally, galangal's analgesic properties help to block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from chronic nerve pain conditions such as sciatica, shingles, and fibromyalgia. Its warming and comforting flavor also promotes relaxation and reduces muscle spasms, further alleviating nerve-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Galangal tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols, shogaols, and paradols, which can help reduce inflammation in the body that contributes to nerve pain.

Analgesic and anesthetic properties: The essential oils present in galangal tea, such as beta-carophyllene and cineole, have analgesic and anesthetic effects, helping to numb the pain and reduce discomfort associated with nerve pain.
Antioxidant activity: Galangal tea's rich antioxidant content can help protect nerves from damage caused by free radicals, which is thought to contribute to conditions like neuropathy.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Galangal's rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving nerve pain when consumed as a tea.
Roots: The roots of the galangal plant contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve-related discomfort, making them an ideal choice for medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest alpinia galanga roots, clean them thoroughly and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 10 grams per serving.
Heat one cup of water in a medium saucepan over moderate heat for about five minutes or until it reaches boiling point.
Add the chopped alpinia galanga root to the boiling water and let it steep for 15-20 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove any remaining solids, retaining approximately 200 milliliters of liquid.
Let the herbal tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature before consuming it, allowing its therapeutic properties to alleviate nerve pain and discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with nerve pain because of its unique blend of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids. These potent antioxidants interact with the body's natural pain-relieving pathways, reducing inflammation and modulating the transmission of pain signals to the brain. By soothing the nervous system, elder tea can provide relief from chronic nerve pain, numbness, tingling, and burning sensations associated with conditions such as sciatica, peripheral neuropathy, and fibromyalgia.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Elder tea's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate nerve pain.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids in elder tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation around the nerves, relieving pressure on them and reducing pain.

Alpha-aminoadipic acid (AAA) content: Elder tea contains a high amount of AAA, an amino acid that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce nerve pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have been traditionally used to relieve nervous tension and promote relaxation, making them an effective ingredient in teas for nerve pain.
Barks: The bark of the elder plant has been shown to exhibit analgesic properties, which can help reduce nerve pain and inflammation when consumed as a tea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean the sambucus nigra flowers ensuring they are free of contaminants.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth removing any solids and sediment.
Allow the tea to cool before consumption, drinking three times a day as needed for nerve pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with nerve pain because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which can contribute to nerve pain. Additionally, licorice tea's flavonoids and saponins may also help to calm the nervous system and reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate nerve pain. As a result, drinking herbal licorice tea may provide relief from chronic nerve pain conditions such as sciatica, shingles, and fibromyalgia.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound found in licorice root, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to nerve pain.

Flavonoids: Licorice tea contains flavonoids like quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing nerve pain and inflammation.

Licoricidin: A bioactive compound present in licorice root, licoricidin has been shown to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate neuropathic pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective for relieving nerve pain.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of licoricidin, an isoflavanone that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a kettle or microwave-safe bowl until it reaches 212 degrees fahrenheit for 3 minutes.
Pour the hot water over the powdered root and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and sediment.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before serving and consuming in moderation up to three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica teas helps with nerve pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation that can cause discomfort and numbness in the nerves. The tea's active compounds, including angelic acid and isoangelicin, also have analgesic properties, which provide relief from chronic pain. Additionally, herbal angelica tea has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, common co-occurring conditions with nerve pain, allowing for a more restful and peaceful state of mind.
remedy_properties: Essential Oils: Angelica tea contains a variety of essential oils, including angelic acid and isoangelicin, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce nerve inflammation and alleviate pain.

Coumarins: The tea's coumarin content has been shown to exhibit antispasmodic effects, relaxing smooth muscle fibers and reducing spasms that can contribute to nerve pain, such as sciatica and neuralgia.

Volatile Compounds: Angelica tea contains volatile compounds like beta-pinene and limonene, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to numb the nerves and reduce sensitivity to pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Angelica roots have been traditionally used to treat nervous disorders, including anxiety and insomnia, due to their ability to calm the nervous system.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the angelica plant contains a high concentration of compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease nerve-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried angelica archangelica root, stems, and leaves.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven or air-dry for 24 hours.
Grind 10-15g of dried angelica archangelica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ground herb in 1 cup boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain.
Allow the tea to cool and drink immediately, or store in an airtight container refrigerated up to 3 days.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nerve pain that are treated with herbal teas are chronic stress, anxiety, and depression. Herbal teas like chamomile, lavender, and valerian root have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression. Other herbs like passionflower and skullcap can also help alleviate restlessness and insomnia, which are often accompanied by nerve pain. These herbal teas promote relaxation and tranquility, providing relief from these common ailments.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for nerve pain are a natural remedy that helps alleviate discomfort and inflammation associated with nerve damage or irritation. The best herbal teas for nerve pain include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Avena sativa, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Hypericum perforatum, Melissa officinalis, Cannabis sativa, and Vitex agnus-castus. If left untreated, nerve pain can disrupt daily life, causing sleep deprivation, mood swings, and reduced productivity, ultimately affecting mental and physical well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with nerve pain is a blend of passionflower, lavender, and ginger. Passionflower's calming properties help to reduce anxiety and inflammation, while lavender's soothing effects ease pain and promote relaxation. Ginger adds a spicy kick that increases circulation and reduces numbness, making it an ideal combination for alleviating nerve pain. Drinking a cup of this tea blend before bed can promote a restful night's sleep and help to manage nerve pain symptoms.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, herbal teas have been found to be effective in alleviating nerve pain due to their anti-oxidant, anti-inflammatory, anti-apoptotic, neuroprotective, and calcium inhibitory actions. The study, which involved an extensive review of 16 herbal plants commonly used for the treatment of neuropathic pain, found that these plants exhibited protective effects on neuropathic pain in various animal and patient models. As a result, herbal teas made from these plants, such as Acorus calamus, Curcuma longa, and Salvia officinalis, can be a useful natural remedy for managing nerve pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique combination of compounds that interact with the nervous system. The roots of the valerian plant contain valerenic acid and other actives that bind to GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing neuronal excitability. This results in a decrease in the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from nerve pain. The sedative properties of Valeriana officinalis tea also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, further alleviating nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps reduce nerve pain by blocking the activity of GABA receptors, which can contribute to anxiety and insomnia.
Valtrate: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related nerve pain.
Isovaltrate: Exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to calm the nervous system and alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the herb's sedative and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: Valerian stems are also utilized for their ability to release valerenic acid, which is then used to create teas that help with nerve pain and insomnia.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are used in some teas due to their lesser-known but still effective ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, although they are not as potent as the roots or stems.
remedy_recipe: Choose the highest quality dried valeriana officinalis roots for brewing herbal tea.
Measure one teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis roots per 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the dried valeriana officinalis roots for 5-7 minutes in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the dried valeriana officinalis roots.
Drink the valeriana officinalis tea immediately to reap its potential health benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids and alkaloids that interact with the brain's neurotransmitters to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. The sedative properties of Passiflora incarnata, commonly known as passionflower, can help alleviate nerve pain by calming the nervous system and reducing anxiety. This can lead to a decrease in pain perception, allowing individuals to find relief from nerve-related discomfort and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Isovitexin: Isovitexin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
Flavonoids (including vitexin and isovitexin): Flavonoids have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which helps to calm nerve pain and promote relaxation.
Harman alkaloids (including harmine and harmaline): Harman alkaloids have a sedative and anxiolytic effect, which helps to reduce anxiety and alleviate nerve pain caused by stress and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: They are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation, making them a popular choice for teas.
Leaves: In some cases, dried and crushed leaves are used to make teas due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and alkaloids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried passiflora incarnata flowers or 2 ounces of fresh flowers.
Steep the flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool to room temperature.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with nerve pain because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two compounds that have been shown to reduce nerve inflammation and promote relaxation. As a result, it can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as sciatica, fibromyalgia, and neuropathy. The soothing effects of the tea can also promote better sleep and reduce stress, further contributing to its pain-relieving benefits. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia tea may lead to long-term relief from nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with nerve pain by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can reduce neuronal excitability and anxiety, thereby alleviating pain.
Linalyl acetate: As a terpene ester, it exhibits anxiolytic and analgesic properties, contributing to its pain-relieving effects by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing inflammation.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce nerve inflammation and oxidative stress, thus providing relief from nerve-related pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make teas for nerve pain due to their calming and soothing properties.

Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make teas for nerve pain as they contain high levels of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make teas for nerve pain as they contain a moderate amount of essential oils, which contribute to their therapeutic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning for optimal oil content and potency.
Rinse 2-3 teaspoons of dried flowers or 1-2 tablespoons of fresh flowers in a fine-mesh strainer.
Steep the flowers in 8 oz boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their volatile oils.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove solids.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa teas helps with nerve pain because it contains a wealth of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that work to soothe and calm the nervous system. The herb's active compounds, such as avenacosides and avenanthramides, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, leading to a decrease in pain and discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate nerve pain and promote relaxation, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to pain management.
remedy_constituents: Fatty acids: Help reduce inflammation and promote healing in damaged nerve tissues, alleviating pain and discomfort.
Phenylethylamine: Act as a natural pain reliever and mood stabilizer, reducing nerve pain by influencing neurotransmitter activity and promoting relaxation.
Avenacoside: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, protecting nerve cells from damage and promoting the regeneration of damaged nerve tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe nerve pain.
Seeds: Used for their high content of omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants to reduce inflammation and promote nerve health.
Roots: Used for their rich concentration of saponins and flavonoids to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of avena sativa roots and 1 tablespoon of dried flowers from a trusted source.
Rinse the roots in cold water for 5 minutes to remove any impurities and debris.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried roots and 1 teaspoon of dried flowers in a tea infuser.
Steep the mixture in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain and serve the tea immediately after preparation to preserve its potency and flavor.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas helps with nerve pain because it contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These active ingredients work by reducing inflammation and blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body. As a result, ginger tea has been traditionally used to alleviate nerve pain associated with conditions like sciatica, fibromyalgia, and neuropathy, providing relief from numbness, tingling, and burning sensations in the affected areas. Its natural properties make it an attractive alternative to conventional pain management options.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain associated with conditions like arthritis and fibromyalgia.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in soothing nerve pain and discomfort.

Volatile oils (including zingiberene and bisabolol): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to nerve pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part, rhyzomes are rich in gingerols and shogaols, compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Similar to rhyzomes, ginger roots are also rich in gingerols and shogaols, making them effective in relieving nerve pain.
Leaves: Ginger leaves, although less commonly used, contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water for herbal tea preparation.
Steep the dried root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes or until the desired flavor is achieved.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the root particles.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired to enhance the flavor and aroma of the tea.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy the health benefits of this herbal remedy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas helps with nerve pain because it improves blood flow to the brain and nerves, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. The tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to calm and soothe nerve endings, reducing discomfort and pain. The flavonoids and terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba also have a neuroprotective effect, which helps to maintain the health and function of nerve cells, promoting overall nerve health and alleviating pain associated with nerve damage or conditions such as neuropathy.
remedy_constituents: Ginkgolides: These sesquiterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce nerve inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to nerve pain.

Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds, including quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-excitotoxic properties, which help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and protecting against nerve damage.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene trilactone has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which help reduce nerve inflammation and promote the health of nerve cells to alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba for teas, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that provide the medicinal benefits.
Seeds: Used in some teas, as they are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help alleviate nerve pain.
Roots: Some teas also use roots from Ginkgo biloba, as they contain compounds that may aid in reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully mature.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 1 to 2 weeks.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ginkgo biloba powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve it hot or chilled after it has cooled to room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas helps with nerve pain because it contains bioflavonoids and hyperforin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the nervous system, alleviating pain and discomfort. The tea also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce nerve pain and promote relaxation. As a result, many people find relief from nerve pain after consuming Hypericum perforatum teas regularly.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
Hypericin: Exhibits strong antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to nerve damage and pain.
Quercetin: Functions as a powerful anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and preventing the release of pain-producing chemicals in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving nerve pain.
Flowers: The flowers contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate nerve pain and inflammation.

Stems: The stems of Hypericum perforatum are also used to make teas for nerve pain, as they contain a combination of flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25-50 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves.
Grind 5-10 grams of the dried material into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of the powdered hypericum perforatum in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool before consumption or refrigerate for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas helps with nerve pain because of its unique composition of bioactive compounds, particularly melissic acid and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce nerve inflammation, which is often a contributing factor to nerve pain. Additionally, the calming and soothing effects of Melissa officinalis teas may help to regulate nerve function and promote relaxation, providing relief from nerve-related discomfort and promoting overall well-being. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, which helps reduce swelling and pain associated with nerve damage.
Luteolin: As a flavonoid, luteolin has potent antioxidant properties that help protect nerve cells from oxidative stress and damage, contributing to nerve pain relief.
Geranial: This terpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by nerve damage and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used as they contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, that provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.
Stems: The stems are used in combination with leaves, as they contain similar bioactive compounds, which contribute to their pain-relieving properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used to add a calming effect to teas, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have a soothing impact on the nervous system, helping to alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers from the plant, making sure they are fresh and free of contaminants.
Dry the harvested melissa leaves and flowers in a warm place for 2 hours or using a food dehydrator for 6 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried melissa leaves and flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discard the solids and let the tea cool for 10 minutes.
Drink the melissa tea immediately, as it is best consumed within 30 minutes of preparation to obtain its full benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa teas helps with nerve pain because of its unique combination of cannabinoids and terpenes. The analgesic properties of cannabidiol (CBD) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) work together to reduce inflammation and block pain signals to the brain. Additionally, the tea's terpenes, such as limonene and pinene, enhance the analgesic effects by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals. This synergy allows Cannabis sativa teas to provide effective relief from nerve pain, often without the need for pharmaceutical interventions.
remedy_constituents: Cannabinoids: They help with nerve pain by interacting with the body's endocannabinoid system, which regulates pain perception and can reduce inflammation and discomfort.
Terpenes (specifically, beta-pinene): They help with nerve pain by reducing inflammation and providing analgesic effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort.
Phenolic compounds (specifically, cannflavin A and B): They help with nerve pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can contribute to pain relief and reduced discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make teas for nerve pain because they contain a high concentration of cannabinoids, particularly THC and CBD, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Buds are used to make teas for nerve pain because they have a high concentration of cannabinoids and terpenes, which work together to provide relief from nerve pain and inflammation.

Flowers: Flowers (or more specifically, the floral clusters of female Cannabis plants) are used to make teas for nerve pain because they contain high levels of CBD and other non-psychoactive compounds that can help reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 7 grams of cannabis sativa flowers and leaves for a single serving of tea.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place for 2 to 3 days to prevent mold.
Grind 1 gram of dried cannabis sativa into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Steep 1 gram of the ground cannabis sativa in 250ml of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve and serve immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus teas helps with nerve pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The herb contains compounds that reduce muscle tension and inflammation, which can contribute to nerve pain. Additionally, Vitex agnus-castus has been shown to have a neuroprotective effect, which can help to promote nerve health and reduce pain. This makes it a popular natural remedy for individuals suffering from nerve pain, including conditions such as sciatica and fibromyalgia. Regular consumption of Vitex agnus-castus tea may provide relief from persistent nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with nerve pain.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the nervous system, providing relief from nerve pain.
Phenylethanoids: Phenylethanoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect nerve cells from damage and reduce inflammation, thus alleviating nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Vitex agnus-castus for tea, as they contain the highest concentration of the bioactive compound aucubin, which helps alleviate nerve pain.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make tea for nerve pain relief due to their content of aucubin and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Vitex agnus-castus are occasionally used to make tea for nerve pain, as they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature dried vitex agnus-castus flowers by hand to ensure quality and freshness.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried vitex agnus-castus flowers for every 8 ounces of water.
Steep the dried flowers in hot water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool before drinking, or refrigerate it for later consumption within a day.
